caption: Figure 1: Overall framework of MMPhysVideo. Left: Our two-stage training framework, which starts with teacher models of parallel branches for joint modeling. Then, we distill a single-stream student model through representation alignment. Right: Our data engine, MMPhysPipe, for physics data curation and multimodal annotation.
description2: The figure shows an overall workflow diagram featuring a two-stage training framework on the left and MMPhysPipe, a data engine, on the right.
description3: Two-panel figure displays a two-stage training framework with parallel branches and a data engine, MMPhysPipe, for physics data curation and annotation.
questions: What is the overall purpose of the framework shown in this image?
How is the data processed and annotated within this framework?

caption: Figure 2: Architecture comparison. Left: Channel-wise fusion used in (Xi et al., 2025a; Chefer et al., 2025) Middle Spatial-wise fusion used in (Huang et al., 2025a; Chen et al., 2025b) Right: Our decoupled design with pixel-wise fusion.
description2: The figure compares three architectures, featuring channel-wise fusion on the left, spatial-wise fusion in the middle, and our proposed decoupled design with pixel-wise fusion on the right.
description3: Four-panel diagram compares channel-wise, spatial-wise, and pixel-wise fusion architectures using block diagrams and labeled components.
questions: How do different approaches to fusion vary in their overall design?
What are the key components used in the depicted architectures?

caption: Figure 3: Overview of MMPhysVideo. Stage I: A dual-stream teacher model with parallel branches is first trained to handle RGB and perception modalities concurrently. Then, we use bidi- rectional control links to enable pixel-wise alignment. Stage II: For inference efficiency, we distill a single-stream student model through relation alginment.
description2: The overview of MMPhysVideo features two stages: concurrent RGB and perception modality handling with parallel branches, followed by pixel-wise alignment via bidirectional control links.
description3: A multi-panel plot displays a dual-stream teacher model and a single-stream student model, connected by relation alignment.
questions: What are the primary stages involved in the MMPhysVideo process?
How does the model architecture change between training and inference?

caption: Figure 4: Overview of MMPhysPipe. We employ a VLM, Qwen3-VL (Bai et al., 2025a), to curate videos with rich physical interactions and generate physical subject descriptions following our chain-of-visual-evidence (CoVE) rule. Subsequently, expert perception models (Carion et al., 2026; Wang et al., 2025b; Xiao et al., 2025) are leveraged to produce multi-granular annotations.
description2: The figure plots the overview of MMPhysPipe, a process combining VLM and expert perception models for curating videos with physical interactions and generating subject descriptions.
description3: Two-panel plot displays video quality assessment, physical richness, and multimodal annotation workflows, using diagrams and text labels.
questions: How does the system leverage visual evidence for describing physical interactions?
What are the key steps involved in creating multi-granular annotations?

caption: Figure 5: Qualitative results. We compare MMPhysVideo with backbones, CogVideoX (Cog) and Wan2.1 (Wan), alongside the advanced physics method, VideoREPA (VR).
description2: The figure compares MMPhysVideo with backbones, CogVideoX, Wan2.1, and VideoREPA, showcasing their performance alongside an advanced physics method.
description3: Two-panel image contrasts various scenes, showcasing a hose spraying, honey drizzling, wine pouring, and a knife severing bread.
questions: What qualitative aspects of physics are being compared in these results?
How do the different methods relate to advanced physics approaches?

caption: Figure 8: Ablation study of our distillation utilizing representation alignment based on CogVideoX- 2B, CogVideoX-5B and Wan2.1-1.3B from left to right, respectively.
description2: Ablation study results are plotted, comparing the performance of three models: CogVideoX-2B, CogVideoX-5B, and Wan2.1-1.3B, utilizing representation alignment.
